strategic_direction.title: Activation of Paid Ads Service Tier
strategic_direction.reasoning: We are already researching targeting and planning a traffic campaign for April 11th. Formally activating this service ensures dedicated budget management and reporting as Jerry scales from flooring to the broader home improvement niche.

strategic_direction.title: Post-Webinar 'High-Ticket' Funnel Expansion
strategic_direction.reasoning: With 78+ registrations recorded previously, we need to move beyond simple follow-ups. We should propose an automated 'application' funnel to pre-qualify coaching leads before they book a call with Jerry.

suggested_agenda.notes: Confirm physical forms are printed and Dan’s 'selling from stage' framework is ready.
Review status of traffic campaigns and slide refinements.
Present new roadmap for home improvement categories and discuss formal Paid Ads activation.
Re-align on overdue email sequences and task delegation.

projected_roadmap.title: SMS Registration Reminders for April 11th Webinar
projected_roadmap.what: Implementation of a 3-part SMS sequence (24 hours before, 1 hour before, and 'Live Now') to maximize show-up rates for the webinar.
projected_roadmap.why_now: With the 'Launch April 11th Webinar Traffic Campaign' active, we are driving traffic, but show-up rates are the bottleneck. SMS typically increases attendance by 15-20% compared to email alone.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 3 hours to set up Twilio/GoHighLevel integration and draft copy.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Approve SMS copy and provide access to the preferred SMS provider.
projected_roadmap.impact: Higher show-up rates lead to more live conversions and better ROI on the paid traffic spend.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: Jerry, we're driving good traffic for the April 11th webinar, but the real win is getting them to actually show up. I'd like to add a quick SMS reminder sequence—it’s the fastest way to bump our attendance by 20% without spending more on ads.

projected_roadmap.title: Flooring to Home Improvement Bridge Campaign
projected_roadmap.what: Creation of a lead magnet and landing page specifically targeting general home improvement contractors (Remodelers, HVAC, etc.) to test the expansion strategy discussed on March 18th.
projected_roadmap.why_now: Since we are already researching targeting for these categories, we need a specific 'bridge' offer to see how well Jerry's flooring success translates to these new audiences before a full-scale launch.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 6 hours: Copywriting for the bridge offer, landing page build, and integration with the email list.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: 1-hour session to record a short bridge video or provide a specific case study outside of flooring.
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Research targeting for home improvement categories
projected_roadmap.impact: Validates the niche expansion and provides data on cost-per-lead for non-flooring coaching clients.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: We've been talking about moving into broader home improvement niches. I want to build out a 'Bridge Campaign'—a landing page and lead magnet that specifically speaks to remodelers—so we can prove the concept and start building that list while we're running your flooring webinars.

projected_roadmap.title: Automated Coaching Application Funnel
projected_roadmap.what: A multi-step survey/application form integrated into the 'Book a Call' flow to filter and qualify leads based on revenue and business size.
projected_roadmap.why_now: As Jerry scales, his time becomes the scarcest resource. The 'Remarketing Book a Call Campaign' will increase volume, so we need a system to ensure he only talks to high-value prospects.
projected_roadmap.effort_b3x: 5 hours to build the logic, conditional tagging, and automated rejection/referral emails.
projected_roadmap.effort_client: Define the 5-7 qualifying questions he wants every prospect to answer.
projected_roadmap.prerequisites: Develop and Implement Remarketing 'Book a Call' Campaign
projected_roadmap.impact: Increases the quality of sales calls, reduces time spent with unqualified leads, and elevates the perceived value of the coaching.
projected_roadmap.pitch_script: As we scale the ads and more people start booking calls, we want to make sure you're only spending time with the most serious, high-revenue contractors. I'm proposing we build an automated application filter so by the time they get on your calendar, you already know they're a perfect fit for your high-ticket coaching.

talking_points.Refine and shuffle webinar slides: Jerry, how are those webinar slides coming along? We want to make sure the flow is tight before the April 11th run.
talking_points.Create and Print Expression of Interest: Have you had a chance to get those Expression of Interest forms printed for the live event? Those are going to be the key to capturing those high-value leads Dan mentioned.
talking_points.Research targeting for home improvement: I've been digging into the targeting for the broader home improvement space—there's a massive audience here once we're ready to bridge over from flooring.
talking_points.Launch April 11th Webinar Traffic Campai: I'm finalizing the traffic campaign for the 11th; I want to make sure Lizz has the email sequences ready to go so the handoff is seamless.
talking_points.SMS Registration Reminders for April 11t: Jerry, I want to add SMS reminders for the webinar—it's a small lift that usually increases the number of people who actually show up by a huge margin.
talking_points.Automated Coaching Application Funnel: Now that we're pushing for more 'Book a Call' conversions, I'd like to put an application filter in place to make sure every lead you talk to is qualified and ready to invest.
